events.title: OSPO Monthly Meetup: Open science as a process, not a product
events.description: This monthly meetup focuses on open science, discussing it as a process rather than just a product.

events.title: Cultural Analytics series: Talk with Bob L. T. Sturm 'I am Troubled'
events.description: A talk by Bob L. T. Sturm as part of the Cultural Analytics series, exploring the computational analysis of cultural phenomena.

events.title: Cultural Analytics series: Discussion with Bob L. T. Sturm
events.description: A discussion following Bob L. T. Sturm's talk in the Cultural Analytics series.

events.title: CRELS speaker series with Julien Migozzi: 'Algorithms of Distinction: Class, Credit Scores, and Property in South Africa'
events.description: Julien Migozzi presents on algorithms and their impact on class, credit scores, and property in South Africa as part of the CRELS speaker series.

events.title: Cultural Analytics series: Talk with Peter Broadwell 'Gesamtkunstvektoren: Perceptual Embeddings for the Performing Arts'
events.description: Peter Broadwell discusses perceptual embeddings for the performing arts within the Cultural Analytics series.

events.title: Cultural Analytics series: Talk with James Abello & Haoyang Zhang 'Graph Cities and their Applications'
events.description: James Abello and Haoyang Zhang present on graph cities and their applications in the Cultural Analytics series.

events.title: Cultural Analytics series: Workshop with Lauren Tilton
events.description: A workshop with Lauren Tilton focusing on methods in cultural analytics.

events.title: Cultural Analytics series: Talk with Taylor Arnold 'Computational Approaches to Pacing and Style in Television Comedy'
events.description: Taylor Arnold discusses computational approaches to analyzing pacing and style in television comedy.

events.title: Cultural Analytics series: Talk with Noah Askin 'The Social Foundations of Creative Influence'
events.description: Noah Askin explores the social foundations of creative influence in this Cultural Analytics series talk.

events.title: Dr. Maxime Polleri: Radioactive Governance: The Politics of Revitalization in Post-Fukushima Japan
events.description: Dr. Maxime Polleri discusses the politics of revitalization in post-Fukushima Japan.

events.title: 2026 Clark Kerr Lectures: Evolving Conditions
events.description: President Eisgruber discusses major trends affecting American research universities, including student debt, competition, and diversity.

events.title: 2026 Clark Kerr Lectures: Contested Missions
events.description: President Eisgruber examines the role of university presidents and the civic mission of American research universities.

events.title: Decolonizing Health Knowledge and Practice
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.description: A presentation reflecting on how public health must adapt as trust erodes, misinformation spreads, and funding tightens.

events.title: Harm Reduction for Public Health
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.description: A session on harm reduction strategies and their application within public health.

events.title: Boeing Exoskeleton Iterations - Succeed, Fail, Learn, Sustain
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.description: A presentation on the iterative development process of Boeing exoskeletons, focusing on success, failure, learning, and sustainability.

events.title: Panel talk: Eating Disorders and Domestic and Sexual Violence
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.description: A panel discussion addressing the intersection of eating disorders with domestic and sexual violence.

events.title: Healthcare Provider Bankruptcies
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.description: A seminar exploring the causes and implications of bankruptcies among healthcare providers.

events.title: Webinar: Retirement Inequality and Older Workers
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.description: A webinar discussing the disparities in retirement and the challenges faced by older workers.

events.title: Food Politics and Policy 2026: A Conversation with Marion Nestle and Marion Standish
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.description: A conversation with leading experts Marion Nestle and Marion Standish on current trends in food politics and policy.

events.title: Zoonotic and Environmental Dimensions of Human Urinary Tract Infections
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.description: This presentation delves into the animal and environmental factors contributing to human urinary tract infections.

events.title: Implementing Equity Incentives: Driving Equitable Care Delivery by Patient Race and Ethnicity
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.description: A discussion on implementing incentives to promote equitable healthcare delivery based on patient race and ethnicity.

events.title: The Inaugural UC Berkeley School of Public Health John Swartzberg Lecture
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.location: 2121 Berkeley Way West (Colloquia Classrooms 1102 & 1104), Berkeley, CA
events.description: The inaugural lecture honoring John Swartzberg, featuring insights into public health challenges and advancements.

events.title: Birthe Panhans | Competition in the AI age: Evolutions in antitrust design and enforcement
events.organizer: Goldman School of Public Policy; Institute of European Studies
events.description: EU competition law enforcer Birthe Panhans explores themes in making antitrust enforcement fit for the AI age.

events.title: 2026 Percy-Synar Awards Ceremony
events.location: Institute of Governmental Studies, 109 Philosophy Hall, UC Berkeley
events.description: Congratulate the 2026 recipients of the Charles H. Percy Undergraduate Grant and Mike Synar Graduate Research Fellowship, with brief presentations of their projects.

events.title: 2026 Wildavsky Lecture: Jens Ludwig
events.location: Banatao Auditorium, 310 Sutardja Dai Hall Berkeley
events.description: The 2026 Wildavsky Lecture features Jens Ludwig, author of 'Unforgiving Places: The Unexpected Origins of American Gun Violence'.

events.description: A graduate writing workshop specifically for students in the UC Berkeley Online MPH program.
